title: Thelypteris dejecta
intro: Thelypteris dejecta, also known as the dejected maiden fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce stress and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris dejecta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites that are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Thelypteris dejecta is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
taxonomy: Thelypteris dejecta is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. The genus Thelypteris is a group of ferns native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. This genus belongs to the order Polypodiales, which is a group of ferns characterized by their divided leaf blades. The family Thelypteridaceae is part of the larger group Ascomycota, but more specifically, it is part of the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns and fern allies. The classification of Thelypteris dejecta is an ongoing subject of research in the field of botany.
